steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: The first sentence uses the vague phrase "certain grains." The second sentence names those grains — maize and wheat — and describes their specific role. The blank must signal that what follows is a precise specification of what was stated generally, not a contrast, comparison, or list item.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each transition against the general-to-specific relationship:
  • Choice A — in particular,: means "to be more specific" and correctly introduces maize and wheat as the precise examples of "certain grains." ✓
  • Choice B — alternatively,: signals an alternative option to the previous information, but maize and wheat are not an alternative — they are the very grains being specified. ✗
  • Choice C — by comparison,: signals a contrast or comparison between two items, but the second sentence elaborates on the first rather than comparing it to something else. ✗
  • Choice D — second of all,: signals a second item in a list, but there is no first item in such a list, and the relationship is specificity, not enumeration. ✗
A is correct.
